submission.prompt: Provide the most optimized Python solution for the 'Two Sum' problem where you find the indices of two numbers that add up to the target.

ceia_justification.claim: The solution is functionally correct but computationally inefficient for large datasets.
ceia_justification.evidence: The code uses nested `for` loops (lines 2-3) causing every element to be compared against every other element.
ceia_justification.implication: The resulting time complexity is O(N^2). If given an constraint array of size N = 10,000, this brute-force approach will trigger a Time Limit Exceeded (TLE) error in production or standard testing environments.
ceia_justification.actionable_advice: Optimize the time complexity to O(N) by introducing a hash map (dictionary) tracking seen values and their indices. This trades O(1) space complexity for O(N) space complexity to achieve massive runtime improvements.
